---
title: "Chart Review"
---

::: {.patient-banner}
# Steven "Steve" Thompson

**54-year-old male** | **Consult:** Language Evaluation
:::

::: {.warning}
**SLP Task:** Review the chart and identify information relevant to a language assessment following left hemisphere stroke.
:::

## Patient Snapshot

| Field | Information |
|------|-------------|
| Name | Steven "Steve" Thompson |
| Age | 54 |
| Date of Admission | Current hospitalization |
| Admitting Diagnosis | Fall with mild hip contusion |
| Primary SLP Diagnosis | Mild expressive and receptive aphasia |
| Prior Stroke | Left ischemic CVA (2 years ago) |

## Chief Concern

::: {.note-box}

Steve was re-hospitalized following a fall at home. Although imaging showed no fractures, his previous left hemisphere stroke resulted in persistent expressive and receptive language deficits. Speech-Language Pathology was consulted to evaluate current communication abilities.

:::

## Past Medical History

| Condition | Status |
|------|---------|
| Left CVA (ischemic) | Two years prior |
| Hypertension | Controlled |
| Osteoarthritis | Chronic |
| Hypothyroidism | Managed |
| Hearing Loss | Moderate; hearing aids available |

## Current Medications

| Medication | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Lisinopril | Hypertension |
| Levothyroxine | Hypothyroidism |
| Acetaminophen | Arthritis pain |

## Social History

| Area | Information |
|------|-------------|
| Living Situation | Lives alone |
| Support | Family nearby |
| Prior Function | Independent with ADLs |
| Mobility | Occasionally used a cane |

## Chart Review Questions

1. Why was Speech-Language Pathology consulted?

2. What medical history increases Steve's risk for aphasia?

3. What factors besides aphasia may influence communication?

4. What additional information would you want before beginning assessment?

## Continue

[Go to Interdisciplinary Notes](interdisciplinary.html)
